info.title: Image Studio
info.text: Composer stays below, generated images stay in the center, and every opened card keeps the full request snapshot.
info.attachmentText: Edit target is the base image, references guide style/content, and mask limits editable areas.
info.flowText: Open any gallery item to inspect image, status, prompt, sent parameters, attachments and raw payload.
info.guidesText: Short step-by-step routes for the actions you will use most often in the studio.
info.guide.generate.title: Regular generation
info.guide.generate.text: Use this when you need a new result from scratch without a source image.
info.guide.generate.step1: Choose a model in the bottom composer and keep the mode set to Generate.
info.guide.generate.step2: Write the prompt, then open Parameters if you need size, quality, background or format controls.
info.guide.generate.step3: Submit the request; the result appears in the gallery and the full payload is saved in its card.
info.guide.edit.title: Image editing
info.guide.edit.text: Use this to modify a specific picture while preserving the source context.
info.guide.edit.step1: Attach a Target image — the studio automatically switches to Edit mode.
info.guide.edit.step2: Add references for style/details and a PNG mask when changes should be limited to an area.
info.guide.edit.step3: Write the prompt as an edit instruction: what to change, what to keep, and how strictly to preserve style.
info.guide.batch.title: Batch generation
info.guide.batch.text: A queue of independent requests with separate prompts, attachments and parameters.
info.guide.batch.step1: Open Batch generation from the bottom composer and create as many requests as needed.
info.guide.batch.step2: For each item, set mode, model, attachments and parameters; the interval is counted between sends.
info.guide.batch.step3: Run the queue: results arrive in parallel and are collected in one batch request card.
info.guide.details.title: Details and reruns
info.guide.details.text: A result card acts as a log: prompt, attachments, parameters, endpoint and raw response.
info.guide.details.step1: Open an image from the gallery to see the central result and the action bar below it.
info.guide.details.step2: Expand details: source intent and attachments on the left, technical trace and JSON on the right.
info.guide.details.step3: Copy prompt/payload or load a single request back into the composer for another iteration.
info.guide.providers.title: Providers and models
info.guide.providers.text: A provider stores endpoints and auth; a model stores the technical model ID and provider link.
info.guide.providers.step1: In Settings, add a provider: generation/edit endpoints, key, headers and timeout.
info.guide.providers.step2: Add one or more models, set their model IDs and attach them to the provider.
info.guide.providers.step3: Save settings and select the active model — it will appear in the bottom composer.
info.guide.probe.title: API checks
info.guide.probe.text: This helps discover which parameters a provider accepts without errors.
info.guide.probe.step1: In the provider settings, run Quick check first to verify that the route responds.
info.guide.probe.step2: Then run the full capability probe; it sends tiny test payloads.
info.guide.probe.step3: After probing, unsupported parameters are hidden and the profile is cached locally.
